title: Acme Logistics — WarehouseIQ login failures
content: Customer Acme Logistics (sector: logistics) reported login failures on WarehouseIQ starting 2026-02-24. Issue summary: users unable to authenticate via SSO or 2FA. Severity P1. Assigned to support engineer Tomas Garcia who triaged the initial report and gathered diagnostic data within the first hour. The customer's data pipeline lead, Priya Ramaswamy, is the technical point of contact on this ticket. Hotfix deployed in version 2.14.3 across all production regions. Customer confirmed resolution within four hours of the rollout. No further action required.

title: Globex Industries — WarehouseIQ login failures
content: Customer Globex Industries (sector: manufacturing) reported login failures on WarehouseIQ starting 2026-02-17. Issue summary: users unable to authenticate via SSO or 2FA. Severity P0. Assigned to support engineer Tomas Garcia who triaged the initial report and gathered diagnostic data within the first hour. Customer's primary use case is real-time inventory reconciliation across three warehouses. Reproduced internally with the customer's payload; engineering team has owned the bug as BUG-6597. Fix in code review now; merge expected in the next two business days.

title: Acme Logistics — RouteOptimizer routing calculation errors
content: Customer Acme Logistics (sector: logistics) reported routing calculation errors on RouteOptimizer starting 2026-05-09. Issue summary: RouteOptimizer producing routes 20-30% longer than optimal. Severity P1. Assigned to support engineer Marcus Webb who triaged the initial report and gathered diagnostic data within the first hour. Their engineering team uses Datadog for observability and forwards all webhooks through there first. Workaround applied: increased timeout to 1800 seconds while the INFRA team investigates the upstream connection pool exhaustion. Engineering owner: Marcus Webb. Follow-up scheduled for end of sprint.

title: Acme Logistics — WarehouseIQ payment processing errors
content: Customer Acme Logistics (sector: logistics) reported payment processing errors on WarehouseIQ starting 2026-01-03. Issue summary: card-on-file charges failing or duplicating. Severity P3. Assigned to support engineer Aanya Sharma who triaged the initial report and gathered diagnostic data within the first hour. The customer's deployment is multi-tenant — they serve 12 downstream brands from one Khora account. Closed as duplicate of TKT-0001. Merged into the parent ticket. Customer asked for proactive notification when the parent resolves; subscribed them to the watchlist.

title: Acme Logistics — WarehouseIQ login failures
content: Customer Acme Logistics (sector: logistics) reported login failures on WarehouseIQ starting 2026-04-03. Issue summary: users unable to authenticate via SSO or 2FA. Severity P2. Assigned to support engineer Lisa Park who triaged the initial report and gathered diagnostic data within the first hour. Customer is currently in a quarterly business review window, so any P1 outage will surface to their CFO. Long-running ticket — transferred to engineering for full root-cause analysis. The investigation revealed a subtle race in the worker queue that surfaces only under sustained load.

title: Acme Logistics — WarehouseIQ data export issues
content: Customer Acme Logistics (sector: logistics) reported data export issues on WarehouseIQ starting 2026-01-22. Issue summary: CSV and Excel exports producing malformed output. Severity P2. Assigned to support engineer Lisa Park who triaged the initial report and gathered diagnostic data within the first hour. Customer's primary use case is real-time inventory reconciliation across three warehouses. Customer-side configuration error — the integration was pointing at the staging API endpoint. Updated public documentation to clarify the env-var distinction. No code change required.

Their engineering team uses Datadog for observability and forwards all webhooks through there first. Closed as duplicate of TKT-0022. Merged into the parent ticket. Customer asked for proactive notification when the parent resolves; subscribed them to the watchlist.

title: Massive Dynamic — WarehouseIQ payment processing errors
content: Customer Massive Dynamic (sector: research and development) reported payment processing errors on WarehouseIQ starting 2026-05-22. Issue summary: card-on-file charges failing or duplicating. Severity P2. Assigned to support engineer Sofia Chen who triaged the initial report and gathered diagnostic data within the first hour. Their engineering team uses Datadog for observability and forwards all webhooks through there first. Workaround applied: increased timeout to 1800 seconds while the INFRA team investigates the upstream connection pool exhaustion. Engineering owner: Marcus Webb. Follow-up scheduled for end of sprint. Related to TKT-0030.

title: Massive Dynamic — InventorySense dashboard rendering bugs

title: Massive Dynamic — CustomsAssist file upload errors
content: Customer Massive Dynamic (sector: research and development) reported file upload errors on CustomsAssist starting 2026-04-01. Issue summary: PDF and image uploads silently truncated at 5 MB. Severity P3. Assigned to support engineer Lisa Park who triaged the initial report and gathered diagnostic data within the first hour. The customer's deployment is multi-tenant — they serve 12 downstream brands from one Khora account. Long-running ticket — transferred to engineering for full root-cause analysis. The investigation revealed a subtle race in the worker queue that surfaces only under sustained load.

title: Pied Piper — RouteOptimizer routing calculation errors
content: Customer Pied Piper (sector: data compression) reported routing calculation errors on RouteOptimizer starting 2026-01-20. Issue summary: RouteOptimizer producing routes 20-30% longer than optimal. Severity P1. Assigned to support engineer Tomas Garcia who triaged the initial report and gathered diagnostic data within the first hour. Customer reported similar symptoms across both their EU and APAC regions in the same week. Root cause identified in the upstream provider's TLS handshake change; escalated to vendor support via case AWS-883421. Customer notified of estimated 48-hour resolution window.
